pattern: With small input bounds the queries can be replayed directly, stepping through each arithmetic progression of indices under a modulus before folding the array with XOR.
hints: Each query touches the indices li, li+ki, li+2ki, ... up to ri - walk exactly those positions.
Apply the multiplications in the given query order, taking the product modulo 1e9+7 after each update.
After all queries finish, accumulate the bitwise XOR across the whole array and return it.

edge_inputs.description: one query covering every index with step one
step larger than range so only the start index is touched
multiplier of one leaves values unchanged
product crosses the modulus on a single index
stride of two over an even-length array

stress.description: 1000 elements with 1000 random queries of varied ranges and steps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 10**9 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    q = n
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randrange(0, n)
        r = rng.randrange(l, n)
        k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        v = rng.randrange(1, 10**5 + 1)
        queries.append([l, r, k, v])
    return [nums, queries]
stress.note: values stay positive so XOR is over non-negative integers below the modulus
